title: Guidance Clarity Act of 2025
plain_summary: This law makes federal agencies put a warning on their guidance documents. The warning must say that the guidance is not a law and cannot be enforced like a law. It also must say that the guidance is only meant to help people understand rules that already exist. The warning has to go on the first page of these documents. This helps people know the difference between real laws and helpful guidance.
last_action_text: Placed on Senate Legislative Calendar under General Orders. Calendar No. 250.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Guidance Clarity Act of two thousand twenty-five.

sections.title: Section 2  Guidance Clarity Statement Required
sections.text: Starting thirty days after the Office of Management and Budget (the government office that helps the President manage federal agencies) creates instructions for this law, all federal agencies must add a clear statement to certain guidance documents they create. This guidance clarity statement must be placed prominently on the first page of the document. The statement must say exactly this: The contents of this document do not have the force and effect of law and do not, of themselves, bind the public or the agency. This document is intended only to provide clarity to the public regarding existing requirements under the law or agency policies. The Office of Management and Budget must create instructions for how agencies should follow this law within ninety days after this law is passed.
